name: Spicy Egg Noodles (Bamee Haeng)
recipeIngredient: Fish sauce (nam pla), or to taste
Kwan's Sweet and Sour Sauce (see recipe)
Dried hot chile flakes, to taste
Shredded barbecued pork, cooked chicken, beef, or shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the fresh whole egg noodles according to package instructions: plunge into boiling water for 4 or 5 seconds, remove and plunge into cold water for 4 or 5 seconds, return to boiling water for 4 or 5 seconds, then drain.
2. In a bowl, combine the cooked noodles with garlic oil, fish sauce, sweet and sour sauce, and dried chile flakes (if desired).
3. Quickly dip bean sprouts in boiling water and drain them.
4. Add the bean sprouts to the noodle mixture.
5. Add one or more of the cooked meats: shredded pork, cooked chicken, beef, or shrimp.
6. Garnish with chopped green onions, fresh coriander leaves, and ground peanuts.
Note: This recipe is inspired by Thai street noodles vendors. Adjust seasonings to your taste. One serving contains approximately 575 calories, 19 grams of protein, 60 grams of carbohydrates, 26 grams of fat (6 grams saturated), 82 milligrams of cholesterol, and 2,840 milligrams of sodium.
